exemplar.title:
How does Dave Eggers use the portrayal of digital communication platforms in 'The Circle' to
comment on the potential for social control in the information age

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does Dave Eggers use the portrayal of digital communication platforms in 'The Circle' to
comment on the potential for social control in the information age

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

versions.error: Durable Object reset because its code was updated.

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and focus throughout.
Research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently linked to the discussion.
Methodology is clearly articulated (close textual analysis plus theoretical lenses) and followed coherently.
weaknesses: Source selection is relatively narrow and lacks peer-reviewed scholarship.
Citations are inconsistently formatted, indicating minor lapses in methodological rigour.
Justification for theoretical framework selection is not fully explained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission maintains a precise focus on Eggersâ representation of digital platforms, with no digressions, and reiterates the guiding intent in introduction, body and conclusion. The research question is explicit, sharply delimited and consistently connected. The methodology is well-stated and adhered to, demonstrating clear evidence of planning and execution. Minor issues in source range and citation formatting prevent a flawless impression but do not materially weaken the overall methodological clarity, justifying the top mark in this criterion.

strengths: Displays solid knowledge of Eggersâ novel with accurate textual evidence and contextualisation.
Use of key concepts (e.g., panopticism, gamification) shows meaningful engagement with theory.
weaknesses: Over-reliance on general web sources (Wikipedia, magazine articles) limits academic depth.
Subject-specific terminology is sometimes misspelled or inconsistently applied.
Source credibility is weakened by lack of peer-reviewed references.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates good knowledge of the novel and core theoretical concepts, applying them coherently to the analysis. However, the depth of scholarly understanding is constrained by an over-dependence on non-academic sources and repeated spelling/inconsistency in technical terminology. These factors place the work solidly in the mid-band for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Argument is consistently focused on the research question, demonstrating good critical evaluation of digital platforms as mechanisms of social control.
Analysis is well supported by textual evidence, with insightful links (e.g., SeeChange cameras to panopticism, TruYou to real-world accounts).
Research material is pertinent and generally relevant, maintaining coherence throughout the essay.
weaknesses: Supporting literature is mostly non-academic, limiting the depth of theoretical engagement.
Evaluation of counter-positions and implications is only partially evident; the conclusion lacks reflection on limitations or future directions.
Some descriptive passages (e.g., McLuhan summary) reduce the overall critical density.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work delivers a well-developed argument with mostly supported analytical insights and maintains relevance to the research question. There is good evidence of critical thinking in linking narrative elements to theory, although the reliance on non-academic sources and limited evaluation of alternative perspectives prevent placement in the top band. Overall, performance aligns with the âgoodâ descriptor for Critical Thinking, meriting 9 out of 12.

strengths: Structure closely follows IB conventions, including clear section headings, logical progression and a comprehensive table of contents.
Layout is clean, with consistent font and adherence to the word limit.
Title page and sectioning support effective navigation of the essay.
weaknesses: Citation formatting is inconsistent, with mixed superscripts and inline URLs.
Page numbers are missing, and some long URLs disrupt line breaks.
Minor run-on sentences and mid-paragraph breaks affect flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all structural and organisational requirements clearly and professionally, with only minor formatting and citation issues. The document is easy to follow and fully satisfies the expectations for an upper-band Presentation score.

strandSelections.reasoning: The method (close textual analysis supplemented by theoretical lenses) is stated and generally followed.  However, source selection is narrowâprincipally the primary text plus Wikipedia, Study.com and one magazine articleâso range and academic rigour are only partly convincing.  Citations are inconsistently formatted, suggesting limited evidence of deliberate, informed selection, placing the work low in the 3â4 band.

Basic layout elements are presentâtable of contents, headings, consistent fontâand the essay respects the 4000-word limit.  Nevertheless, page numbers are absent, citation formatting is erratic (mixed superscript notes and in-line URLs), and some long URLs break across lines, indicating partial rather than full adherence to IB presentation standards.  This fits the 1â2 band with a score of 2.

Research material is limited but generally pertinent: quotations from the novel, basic theoretical texts and a handful of secondary webpages all relate directly to the RQ.  Because much of the supporting literature is non-academic (Wikipedia, Baseline magazine), relevance is adequate but quality is moderate, placing the strand at the bottom of the 4â6 band.

The student states a precise literary focusâEggersâ representation of digital platforms as mechanisms of social controlâand reiterates this purpose consistently in the introduction, topic sentences and conclusion.  The guiding idea of âpotential for social control in the information ageâ is clear and sustained, with no digressions into unrelated technological debates.  Minor lapses in phrasing (âever-growingly interwovenâ) do not impede understanding, so the work aligns best with the âaccurately and effectivelyâ descriptor, albeit not at the very top of the band.

A line of argument is maintainedâEggers warns of creeping social controlâbut evaluation of counter-positions is limited and critical distance from the primary text is only occasionally evident.  The discussion is coherent yet sometimes repetitive, so the work meets âgood argument development with partially critical evaluationâ at the lower end of the 4â6 range.

The essay moves beyond plot summary, linking textual evidence to concepts of surveillance and media theory, and examines language choices such as repetition of âoneâ.  However, many assertions remain descriptive (e.g., listing platform functions) and some claims about real-world parallels lack supporting evidence.  Conclusions are partially but not fully substantiated, fitting the âadequate/partially supportedâ descriptor at 4.

The essay follows the IB EE structural conventions: title page, abstract-style introduction, clear section headings, conclusion and reference list.  Logical progression from theory to analysis is easy to follow, so the structure âclearly meets requirements,â meriting the upper band for this criterion.

The student shows solid knowledge of Eggersâ novel and is able to summarise panopticism and âmedium is the messageâ.  Quoted passages are accurate and usually contextualised.  Nevertheless, over-reliance on general web sources limits the depth of literaryâcritical scholarship, preventing the âcoherent and consistently effectiveâ standard required for the top band.

Key termsâpanopticism, gamification, transparencyâare used in meaningful ways, but repeated spelling errors (âFoucaltâ, âMcluhanâ) and occasional misuse (âMichael Foucaltâs Theory of Surveillance and Power; Foucalts conceptâ¦â) show inconsistency.  Overall usage is âmostly accurateâ rather than âaccurate and consistentâ, warranting the lower half of the 3â4 band.

The research question is explicitly formulated (âHow does Dave Eggers useâ¦â) and tightly delimits the scope to three platforms in The Circle.  All body sections return to this question, showing clear linkage.  Some imprecision in theoretical labels (mis-spelling of âFoucaultâ, âMcLuhanâ) prevents a fully flawless impression, hence 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: The title page clearly presents the investigation topic and session, giving a professional opening to the essay.
The title âAn exploration of social controlâ¦â is focused and communicates the research intent effectively, sustaining clear purpose.
Word count is clearly stated (3986) and falls within the 4000-word limit, meeting IB requirements.
Pages are not numbered in the footer, making navigation harder; add consistent page numbering throughout.
The table of contents is comprehensive and maps sections to correct page references, aiding reader orientation.
Several theorist names are misspelled (e.g., âMcluhanâ and âFoucaltâ) in the contents; correct terminology for accuracy.
The phrase âever-growingly interwovenâ and âportravalâ contain awkward wording and a typo; revise for clarity and precision.
The definition of digital communication relies on a general web source; supplement with peer-reviewed or scholarly references.
Footnote uses Baseline magazine and Wikipedia exclusively; broaden methodology by including academic journals for depth.
The theoretical framework paragraph introduces two theories but lacks justification for their selection; clarify rationale to strengthen focus.
Relying on Wikipedia footnotes undermines academic rigor; replace with seminal works or peer-reviewed articles.
The panopticon theory section is largely descriptive with limited linkage to The Circleâs text; integrate quotes earlier to ground analysis.
The sentence ends abruptly at âEggers makes use of capital letters to signify theâ; complete or restructure for coherence.
Footnote formatting shifts between inline URLs and citation style; adopt a consistent IB-approved citation format.
Insightful link of SeeChange cameras to panopticism shows strong engagement with theory and text.
Combining McLuhan and panopticism on one page blurs structural transitions; separate sections more distinctly.
Lengthy McLuhan summary lacks direct engagement with The Circle; condense theory and focus on textual application.
Citing Wikipedia for core theoretical assertions weakens source credibility; reference original McLuhan text.
The introduction of McLuhanâs theory features extensive run-on sentences; break into shorter statements for clarity.
The discussion of TruYouâs identity effects illustrates McLuhan well and shows strong analytical connection.
The paragraph concludes mid-thought; ensure each analytical section finishes with a coherent closing sentence.
Vivid examination of PartiRankâs community framing shows depth in relating narrative choices to social control.
Transition into the analysis section (âTruYouâ) lacks an introductory topic sentence; add one to clarify purpose.
Repeated superscripts and platform labels disrupt fluency; streamline citation style to maintain analytic flow.
Linking TruYou to real-world Apple ID and Google Accounts is effective in contextualizing the novelâs themes.
Footnote style varies between âpgâ and inline citations; unify style to comply with a single citation format.
Explanation of SeeChangeâs panoptic function is clear and well-supported by textual evidence.
Claims about Maeâs behaviour change lack backing from secondary scholarship; incorporate academic sources on social psychology.
Nuanced description of PartiRankâs psychological impact demonstrates original insight and strong analytical depth.
Consider evaluating alternative interpretations of PartiRankâs societal impact to enhance critical evaluation of the text.
Textual evidence linking Baileyâs âpassion projectâ to corporate expansion is well chosen and insightful.
The conclusion summarises key points but lacks reflection on the essayâs limitations or future implications; add evaluative commentary.
Bibliography entries are inconsistent in style and not fully alphabetized; adopt a single recognized format (MLA/APA) and reorder.
Repeat use of Wikipedia in bibliography should be replaced with more authoritative sources; include original texts or peer-reviewed works.
